title: atraphaxis billardierei subsp. tournefortii
intro: Atraphaxis billardierei subsp. tournefortii, also known as Tournefort's desert heliotrope, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, yellowish flowers and shrub-like growth. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and as a diuretic, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Atraphaxis billardierei subsp. tournefortii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Traditional herbal preparations involve infusing the plant's leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or using the plant's extracts in capsule or tincture form to harness its medicinal properties.
